rulings.Case Title: BENNETT VS ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BHC)*
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary Judgment granted. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact that decedent was exposed to defendant's product, or that defendant's product caused decedent's illness. Objection to Johnson declaration sustained, D'Amico vs. Board of Medical Examiners, 11 Cal 3rd 1. Objection to Ay declaration sustained. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MORRIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/AND OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Judgment, and motion for Summary Adjudication as to negligence and strict liability is denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. Defendant's motion for Summary Adjudiction is granted as to false representation, Concert of action, and, punitive damages. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: WESLEY HOLLIS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Supporting Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and, SAN FRANCISCO TAXICAB COMMISSION'S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment. Motion for Summary Judgment is Granted. Defendants provided plausible reasons for their taxi retirement regulations: safety, cleanliness, and comfort of cabs, improvement of public satisfaction with taxi services, lower emissions, and promotion of the taxi industry generally. Plaintiff failed to show that any of the justifications is irrational. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JENNITHA RICHARDSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice. Motion granted without leave to amend, based on variance between plaintiff's government claim and complaint. See Fall River Joint Unified School District vs. Superior Court, 206, Cal.App.3d 431. There is no reasonable possibility that the defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JOAN B HARTLEY VS. GEORGE VALVERDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Verified Petitionfor Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2009, line 12, PETITIONER JOAN HARTLEY'S Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petitionfor Writ Of Mandate. Grant and Remand for furthr proceedings consistent with this ruling. The Administrative Decision of Officer Medina relfects that she did not properly consider Petitioner's objections to evidence of the Wisconsin Operating While Intoxicated Records, in light of, Draeger vs. Reed, 69, Cal.App. 4th, 1511. =(302/CWW/JW)
